big_picture: India's top policy news today includes Iran reiterating it will not close the Strait of Hormuz, while a Saudi oil tanker arrived in Mumbai. The Odisha Congress moved its MLAs to a Bengaluru resort amid concerns over cross-voting in Rajya Sabha polls. The Enforcement Directorate attached assets worth Rs 581 crore belonging to Anil Ambani-led Reliance firms. SEBI issued a corrigendum to correct legal provisions in its January 2026 notification amending LODR Regulations. The Supreme Court ruled that sufficient cause must be shown to dismiss a public servant without a departmental enquiry, reinstating a Delhi Police officer. The Supreme Court will examine whether the Lokpal Act envisages separate sanctions for chargesheet and prosecution in a plea against Trinamool Congress MP Mahua Moitra.

articles.title: To Dismiss Public Servant Without Dept Enquiry, Sufficient Cause Must Be Shown : Supreme Court Reinstantes Delhi Police Officer
articles.summary: The Supreme Court on Thursday (March 12) observed that the power to dismiss a government servant from service without holding a departmental enquiry cannot be exercised on mere presumption that conducting such an enquiry is not reasonably practicable. The Court added that the decision of the authority to dispense with the requirement of holding an enquiry before dismissing a government servant should be supported by some material. A bench comprising Justices J.K. Maheshwari and Atul S. Chandurka

articles.summary: The Supreme Court issued a judgment on March 11 clarifying how to apply income/wealth tests for OBC candidates whose parents work in Central/State PSUs or private employment. This ruling resolves decades-long confusion and impacts reservation policies, ensuring fair access to benefits. The decision is a major step in refining the creamy layer exclusion criteria for OBC quotas.

articles.title: Supreme Court Upholds Claim Of Candidates With Learning Disability & Mental Illness To Join CAG Auditor Post
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that government authorities cannot reject candidates with benchmark disabilities based on outdated post identification rules. The court directed the appointment of candidates previously denied due to reliance on the 2013 guidelines, emphasizing the use of the latest 2016 disability rights law. This decision ensures compliance with updated disability inclusion policies in government recruitment.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court on Thursday (March 12) held that government authorities cannot deny appointment to persons with benchmark disabilities by relying on outdated identification of posts, and must apply the latest notification issued under the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016. The Ninth Schedule protects laws from judicial review, and this demand highlights ongoing debates over federalism and affirmative action policies in India.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court has taken suo motu notice of illegal sand mining in the Chambal Sanctuary, which threatens the habitat of gharials. The court observed that even areas where Madhya Pradesh's Chief Minister recently released gharials have been affected by illegal mining. This judicial intervention highlights the need for stricter enforcement of environmental laws and protection of wildlife sanctuaries.

articles.title: 3 Year Practice Mandate : Law Colleges Suggest Alternatives For Special-Abled Candidates
articles.summary: Several law universities have suggested broadening the definition of 'practice at the Bar' to include alternative legal experience for persons with disabilities. This follows the Supreme Court's call for suggestions on exempting them from the mandatory three-year practice requirement for entry-level judicial posts. The proposals were submitted to the Court by Senior Advocate Siddharth Bhatnagar.
articles.raw_summary: Several law universities and institutions have suggested that the meaning of “practice at the Bar” be broadened to include alternative forms of legal experience while responding to the Supreme Court's call for suggestions on whether persons with disabilities should be exempted from the mandatory three-year practice requirement for entry-level judicial service posts. Their suggestions have been placed before the Court through a compilation filed by amicus curiae Senior Advocate Siddharth Bhatnaga

articles.title: Madras HC Quashes DGFT EPCG Penalty Order as Hearing Opportunity Was Denied
articles.summary: The Madras High Court overturned a penalty imposed by the Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) for alleged export obligation defaults under the EPCG scheme. The court ruled that the petitioner was denied a fair hearing and ordered the DGFT to provide one. This decision impacts businesses dealing with export obligations and highlights the importance of procedural fairness in regulatory actions.
articles.raw_summary: The Court set aside the DGFT order imposing penalty and customs duty demand for alleged EPCG export obligation default. articles.title: Oil shock, inflation pressures dampen RBI rate-cut hopes
articles.summary: Economists believe India's policy interest rate cuts are unlikely to continue due to West Asia crisis-driven inflation risks and the fading of a favorable base effect. Sustained foreign outflows and a weakening rupee could also force the Reserve Bank of India to reconsider further easing, potentially even a hike.

Government finances may also face pressure from increased subsidies, potentially impacting infrastructure spending and economic growth.

articles.summary: The U.S. has issued a 30-day license allowing countries to purchase Russian oil stranded at sea, which has eased supply concerns and lowered oil prices. This move follows coordinated efforts by the IEA to stabilize global energy markets amid rising tensions in the Middle East. The decision could influence India's energy imports and trade policies.
articles.raw_summary: Oil prices dipped Friday after the U.S. issued a 30-day license allowing the purchase of stranded Russian oil, easing immediate supply concerns. This move follows a larger release from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve and coordinated efforts by the IEA to stabilize global energy markets amidst escalating Middle East tensions and threats to the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Iran tanker attack ‘deliberate, calculated’, Safesea chief says shipping lanes not battle zones

articles.title: Karnataka clears ‘Iva Nammava, Iva Nammava’ Bill: What the proposed law against honour killings seeks to do
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has cleared the 'Iva Nammava Iva Nammava' Bill, which aims to curb honour killings and related violence. The law proposes a minimum five-year prison term for those responsible. This legislation addresses a serious social issue and strengthens legal protections for individuals facing such threats.

The move comes a day after Washington opened a separate investigation into 16 trading partners over excess industrial capacity, signalling a broader trade crackdown.

articles.title: 'These are plans to call women inferior': CJI led-Supreme Court bench rejects plea on menstrual leave
articles.summary: The Supreme Court rejected a plea to mandate nationwide menstrual leave for women, citing concerns that it could reinforce gender stereotypes and harm employment prospects. While voluntary policies are allowed, the court directed authorities to consider the petitioner's representation. The ruling emphasizes potential negative career impacts if menstrual leave were made compulsory.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court has declined to mandate nationwide menstrual leave for women, citing concerns that it could reinforce gender stereotypes and harm employment prospects. While acknowledging voluntary policies are welcome, the court directed authorities to consider the petitioner's representation on the matter, emphasizing potential negative career impacts if made compulsory.

articles.title: In a first, EC appoints officers of SDM level & above as returning officers for Bengal polls
articles.summary: The Election Commission has appointed Sub-Divisional Magistrates (SDMs) or higher-ranking officials as returning officers for 152 assembly constituencies in West Bengal. This move, aligning with national norms, aims to enhance the fairness and impartiality of the upcoming elections. The decision reflects a policy action by the Election Commission to strengthen the electoral process in the state.
articles.raw_summary: In a significant move for West Bengal's electoral process, the Election Commission has upgraded returning officers for 152 assembly constituencies to Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM) or Sub-Divisional Officer (SDO) level and above. This historic decision, aligning with national norms, aims to enhance the fairness and impartiality of upcoming assembly polls by appointing more senior officials.

articles.summary: India's economy faces risks from West Asia conflict affecting oil prices. Real GDP growth is expected at 7.1 percent in FY27. The Reserve Bank of India is likely to maintain interest rates.

articles.summary: Geopolitical tensions in West Asia are creating economic uncertainty for India. Higher oil prices could slow growth and increase inflation. The duration of the conflict will be key to the economic impact.

articles.summary: Scientific evidence suggests that climate change, including rising temperatures and heatwaves, may increase kidney disease risk, particularly among outdoor workers. This could drive future policy action in India to address health and environmental challenges.

articles.title: Arbitration | Belated Jurisdictional Challenge Impermissible After Active Participation In Arbitration Proceedings: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a party cannot raise a jurisdictional challenge in arbitration proceedings after actively participating without objection. The court emphasized that such a challenge cannot be made after an adverse award is passed. This decision sets a precedent for how jurisdictional disputes are handled in arbitration cases in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court has observed that a party which participates in arbitration proceedings without raising a jurisdictional objection at the appropriate stage cannot subsequently raise a technical plea of jurisdiction of the arbitral tribunal upon passing of an adverse award. “A party cannot keep a 'jurisdictional ace' up their sleeve and then claim that filing of the jurisdictional challenge under Section 16 would go back in time and wipe out the past conduct and acquiescence of the party which

articles.title: GERC Proposes Banking Charges of ₹1.5/kWh for Green Energy Open Access Consumers
articles.summary: The Gujarat Electricity Regulatory Commission (GERC) has proposed retaining banking charges of ₹1.5 per kWh for green energy open access consumers until June 30, 2026. The amendment is part of the GERC (Terms & Conditions for Green Energy Open Access) Regulations, 2026, and stakeholders can provide feedback by March 27, 2026. This move impacts solar energy projects in Gujarat, which currently allow daily energy banking between 7 am and 6 pm.
articles.raw_summary: The Gujarat Electricity Regulatory Commission (GERC) has proposed retaining the banking charges of ₹1.5 (~$0.016)/kWh for open access consumers by amending the GERC (Terms   Conditions For Green Energy Open Access) Regulations, 2026. The proposed banking charges will be applicable until June 30, 2026, or earlier if the Commission revises the charges. Stakeholders can share their feedback by March 27, 2026. articles.title: India's Russian oil buys part of effort to be a strong US partner in keeping oil prices stable, Sergio Gor says

articles.title: 'Women will be asked to sit at home': SC says mandatory menstrual leaves would hamper careers
articles.summary: The Supreme Court stated that while private companies can voluntarily offer menstrual leave, making it mandatory could negatively impact women's careers. The court's stance reflects a legal position on workplace policies related to women's health and employment.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court said it would be “excellent” if private companies granted period leaves voluntarily, but making them compulsory would hurt women's careers.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court refused to entertain a Public Interest Litigation (PIL) seeking a nationwide menstrual leave policy. The court's decision is significant as it impacts workplace policies and gender equality discussions in India.

This multi-species conservation plan is a government-led initiative aimed at biodiversity protection. The move highlights the state's commitment to wildlife preservation and may influence future policy decisions in conservation.

articles.title: 'Nobody Will Hire Women': Supreme Court Turns Down Plea On Menstrual Leaves
articles.summary: The Supreme Court rejected a plea for mandatory menstrual leave for women, stating that such a policy could discourage employers from hiring women. Chief Justice Surya Kant said, 'Nobody will hire women if we make such a law.' The ruling has significant implications for workplace policies and gender equality in India.
articles.raw_summary: Mandatory menstrual leave for women could 'adversely impact (their) employment' because employers 'will not hire women if we make such a law', Chief Justice Surya Kant said Friday.

articles.summary: Seafarers in India may still qualify for tax-free foreign salary even if they fail the 182-day NRI test due to crises or travel delays. The CBDT Circular 13/2017 and RNOR status provide legal protections for such cases. This ensures compliance with Indian tax laws while addressing challenges faced by seafarers.
articles.raw_summary: Even if seafarers fail the 182-day NRI test due to crises or travel delays, foreign salary may still remain tax-free. RNOR status and CBDT Circular 13/2017 provide key protection. The post War   182-Day Rule: How Seafarers Can Save Tax on NRE Salary appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: MCA Amends Accounting Standard 22 to Address OECD Pillar Two Global Tax Rules
articles.summary: The Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) has amended Accounting Standard 22 to incorporate provisions related to the OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax rules. The change exempts companies from recognizing deferred tax assets or liabilities linked to Pillar Two income taxes. This amendment ensures compliance with international tax standards and impacts corporate tax accounting in India.
articles.raw_summary: The MCA amended AS 22 to incorporate provisions related to OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax rules. The amendment exempts companies from recognising deferred tax assets or liabilities linked to Pillar Two income taxes. articles.title: TDS Credit Allowed as Legal Heir Declared Deceased’s Income
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that TDS credit must be granted to legal heirs of deceased individuals, even if there is a PAN mismatch. The decision ensures that tax deductions are correctly applied when income is taxed in the heir's hands. This ruling clarifies tax administration procedures for legal heirs.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT ruled that denial of TDS credit due to a PAN mismatch between the deceased and the legal heir is unjustified. Once the income is taxed in the hands of the legal heir, the corresponding TDS credit must also be granted. The post TDS Credit Allowed as Legal Heir Declared Deceased’s Income appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Cheque Dishonour Due to Subsequent Freezing of Drawer’s Bank Account – S.138 NI Act Not Attracted: Karnataka HC
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court ruled that a cheque dishonour due to a subsequent freezing of the drawer's bank account does not fall under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act. The court clarified that the provision only applies when dishonour is due to insufficient funds or lack of arrangement. This ruling has implications for financial transactions and banking regulations in India.
articles.raw_summary: The court analysed whether the reason account blocked falls within the scope of Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act. It held that the provision applies only when dishonour is linked to insufficient funds or lack of arrangement. The post Cheque Dishonour Due to Subsequent Freezing of Drawer’s Bank Account – S.138 NI Act Not Attracted: Karnataka HC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Possession Alone Cannot Decide Title in Property Disputes: Madras HC
articles.summary: The Madras High Court ruled that mere possession does not determine title in property disputes. Even though the defendant proved ownership through a court auction, the court refused to grant an injunction because possession on the date of the suit was not established. This decision clarifies legal standards for property disputes in India.
articles.raw_summary: The court held that although the defendant established title through a court auction purchase, injunction could not be granted as possession on the date of the suit was not proved. The post Possession Alone Cannot Decide Title in Property Disputes: Madras HC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Ahmedabad canceled a penalty under Section 271(1)(c) after reopening an assessment for fresh adjudication. The tribunal ruled that penalties based on the original order cannot stand once the assessment itself is reopened. This decision impacts how tax penalties are applied in India, providing clarity on the relationship between assessments and penalties.

articles.title: Penalty for Non-Compliance Cannot Stand When Assessment Is Completed Under Section 143(3)
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Indore ruled that penalties for non-compliance cannot be imposed if the assessment is completed under Section 143(3) after considering delayed submissions. The tribunal held that such compliance effectively condones earlier defaults. This decision impacts tax enforcement and compliance procedures in India.
articles.raw_summary: The ITAT Indore held that penalty under Section 272A(1)(d) cannot survive where the assessment is completed under Section 143(3) after considering the taxpayer’s delayed submissions, as such compliance effectively condones earlier defaults. articles.raw_summary: Ever since the war broke out, there is a decline of more than 30% per cent in international footfall across all the cities, said Vinayak

articles.title: President Murmu calls for get greater role for women in decision making in farm sector
articles.summary: President Droupadi Murmu inaugurated a global conference on women in agri-food systems and emphasized the need for greater recognition of women's contributions in agriculture. She called for publishing success stories of women in farming to raise awareness and encourage policy action. The demand aligns with broader efforts to promote gender equality in agriculture and could influence future government initiatives.
articles.raw_summary: Inaugurating a three-day Global Conference on Women in Agri-Food Systems, she said the success case studies on women in agriculture should be published widely to ensure more people were aware of their contributions

articles.title: Hospitality sector to get 20% of average monthly Commercial LPG requirement
articles.summary: The government has decided to allocate 20% of the average monthly Commercial LPG requirement to the hospitality sector. This decision was announced in response to a special discussion in Parliament initiated by Leader of Opposition Rahul Gandhi. The move aims to support the hospitality industry, which has been impacted by various factors, including energy supply constraints.
articles.raw_summary: Puri was responding to a special discussion on LPG based on notice given by Leader of Opposition Rahul Gandhi.

articles.title: Wind equipment manufacturers body urges Oil Ministry for reliable LPG supply
articles.summary: The wind energy industry's apex body has urged the Oil Ministry to ensure a reliable supply of LPG, as manufacturers of key turbine components depend on it for high-temperature processes like melting and fabrication. This demand highlights the sector's vulnerability to supply disruptions and could prompt government action to stabilize LPG availability for critical industries.
articles.raw_summary: The wind energy industry’s apex body noted that manufacturers of key turbine components, like castings and towers, heavily depend on Propane/LPG for high-temperature processes such as melting and fabrication

articles.title: Turkish firms continue to work on metro rail contracts despite Ministry’s security directive

The directive, part of 'Operation Sindoor,' has not been enforced, raising questions about compliance and security measures. The article suggests a disconnect between policy directives and their implementation in infrastructure projects.

articles.title: Neighbourhood clinics on crutches
articles.summary: Across Hyderabad’s crowded localities, Urban Primary Health Centres serve as the first point of contact for thousands seeking everyday medical care. Often housed in ageing buildings and run by small teams of doctors, nurses and community health workers, these clinics shoulder much of the city’s routine healthcare burden. But while the city gains prominence for its corporate hospitals and medical tourism, UPHCs continue to function with limited infrastructure and growing patient loads, writes Sid

articles.title: Who is Shantanu Narayen? Outgoing Adobe CEO’s life, career, education and compensation detailed

articles.summary: The Karnataka government has mandated ecommerce companies to pay 1% of their commission to fund gig worker welfare under the 2025 law. The Internet and Mobile Association of India (IAMAI) has urged the state to defer the fee until specifics are defined. This affects gig workers' social security and ecommerce operations in Karnataka.
articles.raw_summary: Per the Karnataka Platform-based Gig Workers (Social Security Welfare) Act, 2025, ecommerce operators are required to pay 1% of their commission, subject to a cap, to fund gig workers’ welfare.

articles.title: Induction cooktops sold out on Amazon, quick commerce apps amid LPG cylinder shortage
articles.summary: Induction cooktops are selling out on Amazon, Zepto, Swiggy, and Blinkit due to a shortage of commercial LPG cylinders. Restaurants and cloud kitchens are shutting down or scaling back operations. The supply disruption may lead to policy interventions to stabilize fuel availability.
articles.raw_summary: Induction cooktops are selling rapidly on ecommerce and quick commerce platforms after a shortage of commercial gas cylinders sparked panic buying. Products are now unavailable on Amazon, Zepto, Swiggy and Blinkit in major cities. The LPG supply pause has also forced restaurants and cloud kitchens to shut or scale down operations.

articles.summary: The Digital India programme is successfully advancing digital inclusion and strengthening the nation's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) through a strategic focus on accessibility and affordability. articles.title: Overseas crypto earnings of moonlighters under I-T lens
articles.summary: In several cases, individuals working in technology and consulting sectors allegedly received part of their compensation in Stablecoins or other cryptocurrencies that were parked in wallets linked with accounts outside India, in low-tax jurisdictions, sources said. The probe also flagged residents investing in overseas crypto exchange-traded funds (ETFs) through offshore brokerage accounts without reporting holdings or gains.

articles.title: Retail inflation climbs to 3.21% in February
articles.summary: Retail inflation in India rose to 3.21% in February, with food and beverage prices contributing significantly to the increase. Core inflation remained steady at 3.4%. Aditi Nayar, chief economist at ICRA, noted that the rise was largely driven by the food and beverages segment. This data could influence future policy decisions by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) and the government.
articles.raw_summary: Sequentially, retail inflation increased 0.1%, while food inflation fell 0.2%. Core inflation remained steady at 3.4%. "The uptick was almost entirely led by the food and beverages (F B) segment, which accounted for as much as 44 basis points of the 47 bps rise in the headline print between these months," said Aditi Nayar, chief economist at ICRA. One basis point is 0.01 percentage point.

articles.title: Food prices push India's inflation to 3.21% in February
articles.summary: Retail inflation climbed to 3.21% in February, primarily driven by a surge in food and beverage prices. This uptick, the second under the revised CPI series, saw food inflation rise significantly, with personal care and precious metals also contributing to the increase. Future inflation may be impacted by Middle East tensions and rupee depreciation.
articles.raw_summary: Retail inflation climbed to 3.21% in February, primarily driven by a surge in food and beverage prices. Future inflation may be impacted by Middle East tensions and rupee depreciation.

articles.title: India's sunflower oil imports halve in February amid West Asia conflict, rising prices
articles.summary: The Solvent Extractors' Association of India (SEA) said average import price for crude sunflower oil rose 17 per cent to USD 1,420 per tonne in February from USD 1,216 a year earlier, while the rupee's 4.2 per cent depreciation over the past year compounded costs for importers and refiners.
articles.raw_summary: The Solvent Extractors' Association of India (SEA) said average import price for crude sunflower oil rose 17 per cent to USD 1,420 per tonne in February from USD 1,216 a year earlier, while the rupee's 4.2 per cent depreciation over the past year compounded costs for importers and refiners.

articles.title: India begins probe into circumvention of countervailing duty on saccharin imports
articles.summary: India has launched an investigation into claims that saccharin imports from China are being rerouted through Thailand to avoid existing duties. The Directorate General of Trade Remedies is examining if Chinese-origin saccharin is being falsely declared as originating from Thailand. If confirmed, India may extend countervailing duties to imports from Thailand. This probe impacts industries like food, beverage, and pharmaceuticals.

If confirmed, India may extend countervailing duties to imports from Thailand. This probe impacts industries like food, beverage, and pharmaceuticals.

articles.title: India’s February retail inflation quickens to 3.21% before Iran war impact hits
articles.summary: India's retail inflation rose to 3.21% in February, driven by geopolitical tensions impacting global oil supplies. This marks the second reading under a revised CPI framework with a 2024 base year, reflecting updated consumption patterns. While food and fuel inflation figures are pending, economists monitor potential imported inflation risks.

While food and fuel inflation figures are pending, economists monitor potential imported inflation risks.

articles.title: Exporters seek priority allocation of LPG and natural gas for manufacturing units
articles.summary: Indian exporters face production challenges due to fuel disruptions. FIEO has requested the government for priority allocation of LPG and natural gas to export-oriented manufacturing units. This is crucial for maintaining production schedules and timely deliveries to international buyers. Ensuring fuel availability will protect existing orders and boost India's reliability as a sourcing destination.

articles.title: Trust, trade and the slow architecture of India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor
articles.summary: Launched in 2024, the Indo-Mediterranean Initiative, embodied by the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC), is evolving as a strategic, layered process. Despite regional conflicts, IMEC is being built through trade agreements, defense cooperation, and infrastructure partnerships, prioritizing trust and resilience over mere connectivity.

articles.title: US begins new unfair trade probe against overproduction in India, 15 others
articles.summary: The US has launched a trade investigation into excess industrial capacity in 16 countries, including India, under Section 301 of its Trade Act. This probe could lead to new tariffs as the US claims overproduction by trading partners displaces American manufacturing and hinders investment. Evidence suggests significant excess capacity in India's petrochemicals, steel, and solar module sectors.

Evidence suggests significant excess capacity in India's petrochemicals, steel, and solar module sectors.

articles.title: India to release product list for faster PN3 FDI approvals
articles.summary: The amended foreign direct investment (FDI) policy guidelines have allowed proposals for investments from China and other land-border countries to include advanced battery components, rare earth permanent magnets, rare earth processing sectors, whose applications will be processed and decided within 60 days.
articles.raw_summary: The amended foreign direct investment (FDI) policy guidelines have allowed proposals for investments from China and other land-border countries to include advanced battery components, rare earth permanent magnets, rare earth processing sectors, whose applications will be processed and decided within 60 days.

articles.title: West Asia conflict could hurt agri input availability: UPL Executive
articles.summary: Geopolitical tensions in West Asia are increasing costs for agricultural inputs like fertilizers, with prices rising 50-80%. While current inventories ensure stable supply for the upcoming kharif season, sustained disruptions could force policy interventions to stabilize prices and ensure food security in India.
articles.raw_summary: Geopolitical tensions and disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz are driving up agricultural input costs, with fertilizer prices already surging 50-80%. While shipping, insurance, and energy costs have climbed, immediate agrochemical availability for the upcoming kharif season remains stable due to pre-built inventories.

articles.title: Iran war impact: Govt mulling diversion of stranded cargo to new markets
articles.summary: India is assessing the diversion of export cargoes stranded by the West Asia crisis to alternative markets. The commerce ministry is seeking details from goods exporters on rerouting options and has also reached out to services exporters to gauge potential risks to trade, including movement restrictions and technology reliance.
articles.raw_summary: India is assessing the diversion of export cargoes stranded by the West Asia crisis to alternative markets. The commerce ministry is seeking details from goods exporters on rerouting options and has also reached out to services exporters to gauge potential risks to trade, including movement restrictions and technology reliance.

articles.title: Centre holds back on fuel exports ban due to logistics, tax hurdles as West Asia conflict rages
articles.summary: India is not banning fuel exports despite global supply issues. Logistical hurdles, contractual obligations with overseas buyers, and tax waivers for export-only refineries are key reasons. Reliance Industries and Nayara Energy are major exporters. China and Thailand have curbed their exports. India aims to avoid adding to global market turbulence. Domestic refiners prefer selling to state retailers for better returns.

articles.title: Private sector optimism rises to decade high on strong demand
articles.summary: With the dawn of a new year on the horizon, Indian firms are brimming with optimism. Consumer demand remains robust, driving companies to invest significantly in innovative projects and R D. Yet, the dark cloud of rising oil prices threatens to dampen this enthusiasm. Nonetheless, this surge of positivity is the strongest we've witnessed in ten years.

articles.title: Eased FDI norms balance capital inflows and strategic safeguards: Experts
articles.summary: India's relaxed FDI norms for land-bordering countries aim to attract capital and technology while safeguarding interests. Experts highlight the pragmatic balance achieved, with a 60-day expedited approval for specified sectors and clarity on beneficial ownership, fostering supply-chain integration and boosting manufacturing.

articles.title: FDI easing not for Chinese firms; to benefit entities with minority Chinese holding
articles.summary: India has eased foreign investment rules. Overseas firms with up to ten percent Chinese shareholding can now invest automatically. Companies from China and nations sharing land borders with India will get faster approvals for investments in key manufacturing sectors. This aims to boost domestic production and attract foreign capital. The government is streamlining processes for greater certainty for investors.

articles.title: India’s GDP, inflation at risk amid West Asia tensions, oil price surge: Economists
articles.summary: India's economic growth faces a potential slowdown next financial year due to ongoing West Asian conflict and rising oil prices. Economists warn inflation could also accelerate if tensions persist. The impact hinges on crude oil prices and the conflict's duration. Higher fuel costs may affect consumers, while oil companies might absorb initial burdens. This scenario could limit interest rate cuts.

This policy change eases cross-border deal-making.

This revision comes in light of adopting a new base year for GDP calculations, providing a refreshed perspective on the country's economic dynamics.

The Strait remains effectively closed, which could disrupt global oil supplies and impact India's energy imports and trade policies.
